mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic confidence improves.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a critical threshold, it signals significant economic distress and reduced consumer confidence, leading to expectations of lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High unemployment historically precedes or accompanies market downturns
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 6.0% but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth combined with stable or declining consumer price inflation indicates healthy consumer demand without overheating, creating an ideal environment for corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month average growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:Strong demand and low inflation are favorable for equities
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained reduction in the federal funds rate by the Federal Reserve signals an easing monetary policy, which lowers borrowing costs for companies and consumers, stimulating economic activity and boosting equity valuations through lower discount rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary easing typically supports equity markets
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines as specified, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month increase in housing starts indicates strengthening residential investment and broader economic confidence, which typically precedes increased demand for related goods and services, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: If HOUST rises by more than 5% MoM, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, signaling a healthy supply side of the economy that supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production growth is a sign of economic strength
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in employment and retail sales signifies robust consumer health and spending power, directly translating into higher corporate revenues across various sectors and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer strength drives economic growth and equity performance
failure_condition: If both PAYEMS and RSAFS conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and often leads to tighter monetary policy, all of which negatively impact corporate profitability and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation is generally detrimental to equity valuations
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 10-year rolling window.
